response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with genuine developer-tooling passion and even direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI), but the role's core stack is absent: Java appears only peripherally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, which are hard requirements for building enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE features. His strongest recent work is in TypeScript/Node and DLang, so despite solid seniority and craftsmanship, the JVM-ecosystem gap makes him a poor fit for this specific position.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored developer tools (test runner, assertion library, MCP code generators)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops and community teaching
Experience with complex, large-scale systems, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated (side mentions from 2016–2018), with no deep JVM internals work
Recent career focus (TypeScript/Node, full-stack product work) diverges from enterprise JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling interest
